items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: BOJ hikes as expected, taper pause ahead
items.summary: BOJ hikes to 1%, pauses bond taper from April 2027 and flags inflation overshoot risk Bank of Japan 25bp rate hike to 1%, as widely expected Goldman cuts Brent forecast to $80 for 2026, $75 for 2027 on Hormuz deal China May data: industrial output beats but retail sales post first fall since 2022 BOJ set to hit 1% but vote split, bond taper pause and July signals are the real story China data May 2026: Industrial output +4.5% y/y (expected +4.2%) China house prices May 2026 -3.5% y/y (prior -3.5%) Oil price path to the $60s pre-war levels could take years despite Hormuz deal, analysts PBOC sets USD/ CNY mid-point today at 6.8108 (vs. estimate at 6.7605) JGBs steady, Nikkei eases from record high as BOJ rate decision looms GM in talks with Lockheed to make weapons parts as Pentagon seeks to restock Analysts back Iran deal as oil price catalyst but warn peace dividend won't be instant BOJ hike certain, RBA and Fed on hold as Iran deal reshapes central bank outlook New Zealand data: May 2026 Food Price Inflation +1.0% m/m (prior flat at 0%) Hedge funds dust off pre-war playbooks as Iran deal reshapes market outlook - noodle pivot Barclays sees gold hitting $4,900 as Iran-driven correction fades Bank of Japan today - preview - set to lift rates to 31-year high as Iran deal clouds path Explosions heard in the Strait of Hormuz Hormuz reopening road map: mines, insurance and stranded ships slow the path for oil flow Hormuz reopening framework a fragile first step, says S&P Global - BOJ hikes to 31-year high as Iran deal lifts regional sentiment Bank of Japan raised its policy rate to 1%, a 31-year high, and announced a pause in JGB tapering from April 2027; the yen barely moved with USD/JPY holding above 160.00 The Nikkei and Topix dipped earlier in the session before bouncing on the BOJ decision headline; South Korean shares extended gains tracking Wall Street's Iran deal rally Goldman Sachs cut its Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $80 from $90 and its 2027 average to $75 from $80, bringing forward Gulf export normalisation to end-July China's May data showed industrial output beating at 4.5% but retail sales posting their first annual decline since the pandemic, down 0.6%, while fixed asset investment deteriorated sharply China's new home prices fell at a slightly faster monthly pace in May, with the property sector continuing to grapple with fragile demand despite tentative stabilisation in larger cities The Bank of Japan delivered its widely anticipated rate hike on Tuesday, lifting its policy rate to 1% from 0.75% and taking borrowing costs to their highest level since 1995. The decision was accompanied by a confirmation that the BOJ will pause its programme of monthly JGB purchase reductions from April 2027, fixing the buying pace at around 2 trillion yen per month. The inflation outlook was characterised in notably direct terms, with the BOJ flagging upside risk to its 2% CPI target and projecting a year-on-year increase clearly above that level in the period ahead. The yen's reaction was muted. USD/JPY held above 160.00 in the wake of the decision, a reminder that the market has long argued the currency needs real economy momentum and a credible path of sustained tightening rather than incremental moves to stage a durable recovery. The Nikkei and Topix had drifted lower ahead of the announcement before bouncing on the decision headline, with both indices ending the session off Monday's record highs. Broader regional sentiment remained constructive, underpinned by the US-Iran peace framework. South Korean shares extended the prior session's gains, tracking Wall Street's overnight rally as the deal continued to lift risk appetite across Asian markets. Goldman Sachs added further weight to the oil market's repricing, cutting its Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $80 per barrel from $90 and its 2027 average to $75 from $80. The bank brought forward its assumption for Persian Gulf export normalisation to end-July from end-August, reflecting growing confidence in the pace of Hormuz reopening. It was Goldman's second downward revision to its oil price forecasts in a week. China's data gave markets more to digest. Industrial output rose a stronger-than-expected 4.5% in May, buoyed by AI-driven export demand, but retail sales fell 0.6% year on year, their first decline since the pandemic. Fixed asset investment contracted 4.1% in the year to date, well below forecasts, and property investment extended its deterioration with new home prices falling at a slightly faster monthly pace. Larger cities showed tentative signs of stabilisation but the broader sector remains under pressure from weak household borrowing appetite and subdued consumer confidence. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 日本央行将利率上调至1%，自2027年4月起暂停缩减购债规模，并警示通胀超调风险 日本央行如市场普遍预期般将利率上调25个基点至1% 高盛下调2026年布伦特原油价格预测至80美元， 2027年降至75美元（基于霍尔木兹海峡协议） 中国5月数据：工业产出超预期，但零售额录得2022年以来首次下滑 日本央行料将达成1%通胀目标，但投票结果分歧，暂停缩减购债及7月政策信号才是关键 中国2026年5月数据： 工业产出同比+4.5%（预期+4.2%） 中国2026年5月房价同比-3.5%（前值-3.5%） 分析师称，尽管霍尔木兹协议达成，油价回归战前60美元区间的进程仍可能耗时数年 中国人民银行今日将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8108（较 （预计为6.7605）日本国债持稳，日经指数自历史高位回落，因日本央行即将公布利率决议 通用汽车正与洛克希德公司商谈生产武器零部件事宜，因五角大楼寻求补充库存 分析师认为伊朗核协议将推动油价上涨，但警告和平红利不会立竿见影 日本央行加息已成定局，澳大利亚央行和美联储维持利率不变，因伊朗核协议重塑央行政策前景 新西兰数据： 2026年5月食品价格通胀环比+1.0%（前值持平于0%） 对冲基金重启战前策略，伊朗协议重塑市场前景——面条转向 巴克莱预计金价将触及4,900美元，因伊朗引发的调整逐渐消退 日本央行今日政策预览——料将加息至31年高位，伊朗协议令前景蒙上阴影 霍尔木兹海峡传出爆炸声 霍尔木兹海峡重开路线图： 矿区、保险及滞留船只阻碍石油流通 标普全球称霍尔木兹海峡重开框架仅是脆弱的第一步 - 伊朗协议提振区域市场情绪，日央行将利率上调至31年高位 日本央行将政策利率上调至1%（31年新高），并宣布自2027年4月起暂停缩减日本国债购买规模； 日元汇率基本持平，美元兑日元维持在160.00上方 日经指数和东证指数早盘一度下跌，随后在日央行决议消息公布后反弹；韩国 受华尔街因伊朗核协议达成为主导的涨势带动，股市延续涨势。高盛将2026年第四季度布伦特原油价格预测从90美元下调至80美元，并将2027年平均价格预测从80美元下调至75美元，同时将海湾地区出口恢复正常的时间提前至7月底。中国5月数据显示，工业产出同比增长4.5%，表现优于预期，但零售额同比下降0.6%，创疫情以来首次同比下滑， 而固定资产投资则急剧恶化。中国5月新房价格环比跌幅略有扩大，尽管大城市房价已出现初步企稳，但房地产行业仍面临需求疲软的困境。日本央行周二如市场广泛预期般加息，将政策利率从0.75%上调至1%，使借贷成本升至1995年以来的最高水平。此次决定同时确认，日本央行将从2027年4月起暂停每月缩减日本国债购买规模的计划，将月度购买规模固定在约2万亿日元。日本央行对通胀前景的表述尤为直白，指出其2%的CPI目标面临上行风险，并预测未来一段时间内同比涨幅将明显高于该水平。 日元的反应较为温和。在决议公布后，美元兑日元汇率维持在160.00上方，这提醒市场，市场长期以来一直认为，日元要实现持久复苏，需要依靠实体经济的动能以及可信的持续紧缩路径，而非渐进式调整。日经指数和东证指数在公告发布前一度走低，但在决议消息出炉后反弹，但两指数收盘时均较周一创下的历史高点有所回落。受美伊和平框架支撑，区域整体市场情绪依然积极。韩国股市延续了前一交易日的涨势，跟随隔夜华尔街的反弹走势，该协议持续提振了亚洲市场的风险偏好。高盛进一步强化了油市重新定价的预期，将2026年第四季度布伦特原油价格预测从每桶90美元下调至80美元，并将2027年平均价格预测从80美元下调至75美元。该行将波斯湾出口恢复正常的预期时间从8月底提前至7月底，反映出对霍尔木兹海峡重开进程的信心日益增强。这是高盛一周内第二次下调油价预测。中国发布的数据给市场带来了更多需要消化的信息。 受人工智能驱动的出口需求提振，5月份工业产出同比增长4.5%，增幅超出预期；但零售额同比下降0.6%，这是自疫情以来首次出现下滑。今年以来，固定资产投资同比萎缩4.1%，远低于预期，房地产投资持续恶化，新房价格环比跌幅略有扩大。大城市虽显现出初步企稳迹象，但受制于家庭借贷意愿低迷及消费者信心疲软，房地产行业整体仍面临压力。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。

the yen barely moved with USD/JPY holding above 160.00 The Nikkei and Topix dipped earlier in the session before bouncing on the BOJ decision headline; South Korean shares extended gains tracking Wall Street's Iran deal rally Goldman Sachs cut its Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $80 from $90 and its 2027 average to $75 from $80, bringing forward Gulf export normalisation to end-July China's May data showed industrial output beating at 4.5% but retail sales posting their first annual decline since the pandemic, down 0.6%, while fixed asset investment deteriorated sharply China's new home prices fell at a slightly faster monthly pace in May, with the property sector continuing to grapple with fragile demand despite tentative stabilisation in larger cities The Bank of Japan delivered its widely anticipated rate hike on Tuesday, lifting its policy rate to 1% from 0.75% and taking borrowing costs to their highest level since 1995. The decision was accompanied by a confirmation that the BOJ will pause its programme of monthly JGB purchase reductions from April 2027, fixing the buying pace at around 2 trillion yen per month. The inflation outlook was characterised in notably direct terms, with the BOJ flagging upside risk to its 2% CPI target and projecting a year-on-year increase clearly above that level in the period ahead. The yen's reaction was muted. USD/JPY held above 160.00 in the wake of the decision, a reminder that the market has long argued the currency needs real economy momentum and a credible path of sustained tightening rather than incremental moves to stage a durable recovery. The Nikkei and Topix had drifted lower ahead of the announcement before bouncing on the decision headline, with both indices ending the session off Monday's record highs. Broader regional sentiment remained constructive, underpinned by the US-Iran peace framework. South Korean shares extended the prior session's gains, tracking Wall Street's overnight rally as the deal continued to lift risk appetite across Asian markets. Goldman Sachs added further weight to the oil market's repricing, cutting its Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $80 per barrel from $90 and its 2027 average to $75 from $80. The bank brought forward its assumption for Persian Gulf export normalisation to end-July from end-August, reflecting growing confidence in the pace of Hormuz reopening. It was Goldman's second downward revision to its oil price forecasts in a week. China's data gave markets more to digest. Industrial output rose a stronger-than-expected 4.5% in May, buoyed by AI-driven export demand, but retail sales fell 0.6% year on year, their first decline since the pandemic. Fixed asset investment contracted 4.1% in the year to date, well below forecasts, and property investment extended its deterioration with new home prices falling at a slightly faster monthly pace. Larger cities showed tentative signs of stabilisation but the broader sector remains under pressure from weak household borrowing appetite and subdued consumer confidence. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 央行政策预期变化可能提升主要货币对波动，杠杆仓位的保证金与回撤压力上升。
items.impactEn: Shifts in central-bank expectations may increase major-pair volatility and raise margin/drawdown pressure on leveraged positions.

items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 15 Jun: Dollar mixed as markets eye key rate decisions
items.summary: At the close: Not the best finish to the day Shares of SpaceX rise 16% as retail piles in Crude oil sets the topside risk with the run lower today Netanyahu: We will remain in Lebanon as long as we need to Trump/US Senior Official and Trump: Strait of Hormuz will be fully open on Friday The copper market is tighter than you think -- Scotia Israeli drone strike in southern Lebanon kills driver NAHB US June home builder sentiment 35 vs 37 expected Four markets that are poised to celebrate the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz US May industiral production +0.1% vs +0.2% expected US June Empire Fed +5.7 vs +14.0 expected Canada April manufacturing sales +4.2% vs +4.5% expected Canada May housing starts 261.4K vs 278.4K prior The USD is lower to start the new week as Iran deal dominates and leads the markets. investingLive European session wrap: Oil prices sink as markets cheer US-Iran agreement The U.S. dollar started the North American session on the defensive but ended the day mixed, falling against the EUR, GBP, CHF, and AUD while gaining versus the CAD, JPY, and NZD. Improving sentiment in the Middle East weighed on the greenback as investors embraced a more risk-friendly tone following reports that the United States and Iran have reached a framework peace agreement that is expected to be formally signed on Friday. According to President Trump, the agreement includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade of Iran, a commitment that Iran will not possess a nuclear weapon, and strict enforcement mechanisms. Additional details are expected later this week. With geopolitical tensions easing, market attention is shifting toward a busy week for central banks. The Federal Reserve is expected to leave rates unchanged when it announces its decision on Wednesday, but investors will be focused on Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting at the helm and any clues regarding the future policy bias. Before the Fed decision, the Bank of Japan is widely expected to raise its policy rate by 25 basis points to 1.00% from 0.75%. Market pricing and economist surveys strongly favor a hike as inflation pressures continue to prove more persistent. The BoJ's April meeting featured a hawkish 6-3 vote split, with three members already favoring an immediate rate increase. Since then, inflation forecasts have moved higher and policymakers have expressed growing concern about upside inflation risks. Markets will also be watching for guidance on bond purchases. The expectation is that the BoJ may signal a slower pace of bond-buying tapering beginning in fiscal 2027 while leaving the door open for additional rate hikes later this year. Current market pricing points to rates reaching approximately 1.25% by the fourth quarter. With Governor Kazuo Ueda sidelined due to hospitalization, Deputy Governor Shinichi Uchida is expected to lead the post-meeting press conference. Elsewhere, the Reserve Bank of Australia is expected to leave rates unchanged when it announces its decision Tuesday evening U.S. time. On Thursday, both the Swiss National Bank and the Bank of England are also expected to maintain current policy settings. Economic data released today was mixed. The June Empire State Manufacturing Index fell to 5.7 from 19.6 in May, well below the 14.0 estimate. While the reading remains in expansion territory, it signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing activity in the New York region. New orders and employment remained positive, but growth momentum weakened and price pressures persisted. Industrial production rose 0.1% in May, below expectations for a 0.2% increase. Manufacturing output was unchanged following a strong 0.7% gain in April, suggesting industrial activity continues to expand but at a more modest pace. Strength in mining and AI-related technology production helped offset softer areas of manufacturing, while capacity utilization edged higher to 76.2%. Housing data was also softer than expected. The NAHB Housing Market Index fell to 35 in June from 37 in May, missing expectations. Elevated mortgage rates, affordability concerns, and higher construction costs continue to weigh on builder confidence. Sentiment has now remained below the key 50 level for more than two years, with many builders increasingly relying on incentives and price reductions to attract buyers. U.S. equities rallied sharply as investors welcomed the easing of Middle East tensions. The Nasdaq led the advance, climbing more than 3%. • Dow Jones Industrial Average: 51,676.18, +469.07 points (+0.92%) — a new record close • S&P 500: 7,554.28, +122.83 points (+1.65%) — within reach of the record close of 7,609.77 set on June 2 • Nasdaq Composite: 26,683.94, +795.10 points (+3.07%) — still below its record close of 27,093.90 U.S. Treasury yields were mixed, with shorter maturities modestly lower and longer-term yields moving higher: • 2-year: 4.070%, -1.4 basis points • 5-year: 4.193%, -1.7 basis points • 10-year: 4.475%, -1.0 basis points • 30-year: 4.976%, +3.5 basis points In commodities, crude oil fell sharply as geopolitical risk premiums were reduced. WTI crude declined $3.54, or 4.17%, to $81.34 after trading as low as $79.70 before rebounding. Precious metals moved higher, benefiting from lower short-term yields and ongoing demand for hard assets: • Gold: +$91 (+2.15%) to $4,309 • Silver: +$1.90 (+2.82%) to $69.93 This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 收盘：今日收盘表现不尽如人意 SpaceX股价大涨16%，散户资金涌入 原油价格今日下行，上行风险显现 内塔尼亚胡： 只要有必要，我们将继续驻留黎巴嫩 特朗普/美国高级官员及特朗普：霍尔木兹海峡将于周五全面开放 铜市紧俏程度超乎想象——丰业银行 以色列无人机袭击黎巴嫩南部致司机身亡 美国全国住宅建筑商协会6月建筑商信心指数35，预期37 四类市场有望因霍尔木兹海峡重开而受益 美国5月工业生产+0.1% （预期+0.2%） 美国6月纽约联储指数+5.7（预期+14.0） 加拿大4月制造业销售额+4.2%（预期+4.5%） 加拿大5月住房开工量26.14万套（前值27.84万套） 随着伊朗协议主导并引领市场，美元在新一周开局走低。investingLive欧洲时段总结： 油价下跌，市场对美伊协议反应积极 美元在北美时段初段承压，但收盘涨跌互现，对欧元、英镑、瑞士法郎和澳元走低，而对加元、日元和新西兰元则走高。 中东局势好转令美元承压，因有报道称美国与伊朗已达成框架和平协议，预计将于周五正式签署，投资者因此转为更倾向于风险资产。据特朗普总统称，该协议包括重开霍尔木兹海峡、解除美国对伊朗的海军封锁、伊朗承诺不拥有核武器，以及严格的执行机制。更多细节预计将于本周晚些时候公布。随着地缘政治紧张局势缓解，市场关注点正转向央行们忙碌的一周。预计美联储将在周三宣布决定时维持利率不变，但投资者将关注美联储主席凯文·沃什上任后的首次会议，以及任何关于未来政策倾向的线索。在美联储决议之前，市场普遍预期日本央行将把政策利率从0.75%上调25个基点至1.00%。随着通胀压力持续显现出顽固性，市场定价和经济学家调查均强烈支持加息。 日本央行4月份的会议投票结果呈现鹰派倾向，以6票对3票通过决议，其中3名委员已主张立即加息。此后，通胀预期有所上升，政策制定者对通胀上行风险的担忧日益加剧。市场还将密切关注有关债券购买的政策指引。市场预期日本央行可能会暗示从2027财年开始放缓缩减购债的步伐，同时为今年晚些时候进一步加息留有余地。当前市场定价显示，到第四季度利率将升至约1.25%。由于行长上田和男因住院而缺席，预计副行长内田信一将主持会后新闻发布会。此外，预计澳大利亚储备银行将在美国时间周二晚间宣布维持利率不变。周四，瑞士国家银行和英格兰银行预计也将维持当前政策不变。今日公布的经济数据喜忧参半。6月纽约联储制造业指数从5月的19.6降至5.7，远低于14.0的预期值。 尽管该指数仍处于扩张区间，但这表明纽约地区的制造业活动显著放缓。新订单和就业情况依然保持正增长，但增长势头减弱，且价格压力持续存在。5月份工业生产增长0.1%，低于预期0.2%的增幅。制造业产出在4月份强劲增长0.7%后持平，表明工业活动仍在扩张，但增速有所放缓。采矿业和人工智能相关技术生产的强劲表现，在一定程度上抵消了制造业其他领域表现疲软的影响，而产能利用率小幅上升至76.2%。住房数据也弱于预期。全美住宅建筑商协会（NAHB）住房市场指数6月从5月的37降至35，未达预期。居高不下的抵押贷款利率、购房负担能力担忧以及建筑成本上升，继续拖累建筑商信心。该指数已连续两年多徘徊在关键的50点以下，许多建筑商正越来越多地依赖优惠政策和降价来吸引买家。随着投资者对中东局势缓和的积极反应，美国股市大幅上涨。纳斯达克指数领涨，涨幅超过3%。 • 道琼斯工业平均指数：51,676.18点，上涨469.07点（+0.92%）——创下收盘新高 • 标普500指数：7,554.28点，上涨122.83点（+1.65%）——距离6月2日创下的7,609.77点收盘纪录仅一步之遥 • 纳斯达克综合指数：26,683.94，上涨795.10点（+3.07%）——仍低于27,093.90点的收盘历史高点 美国国债收益率涨跌不一，短期收益率小幅下行，长期收益率则有所上升： • 2年期：4.070%，-1.4个基点 • 5年期：4.193%，-1.7个基点 • 10年期：4.475%，-1.0个基点 • 30年期：4.976%，+3.5个基点在大宗商品方面，随着地缘政治风险溢价的降低，原油价格大幅下跌。西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）下跌3.54美元，跌幅4.17%，收于81.34美元，盘中一度跌至79.70美元后反弹。受短期收益率走低及对实物资产持续需求的推动，贵金属价格上涨： • 黄金：上涨91美元（+2.15%），报4,309美元 • 白银：上涨1.90美元（+2.82%），报69.93美元 本文由investinglive.com的Greg Michalowski撰写。
items.summaryEn: At the close: Not the best finish to the day Shares of SpaceX rise 16% as retail piles in Crude oil sets the topside risk with the run lower today Netanyahu: We will remain in Lebanon as long as we need to Trump/US Senior Official and Trump: Strait of Hormuz will be fully open on Friday The copper market is tighter than you think -- Scotia Israeli drone strike in southern Lebanon kills driver NAHB US June home builder sentiment 35 vs 37 expected Four markets that are poised to celebrate the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz US May industiral production +0.1% vs +0.2% expected US June Empire Fed +5.7 vs +14.0 expected Canada April manufacturing sales +4.2% vs +4.5% expected Canada May housing starts 261.4K vs 278.4K prior The USD is lower to start the new week as Iran deal dominates and leads the markets. investingLive European session wrap: Oil prices sink as markets cheer US-Iran agreement The U.S. dollar started the North American session on the defensive but ended the day mixed, falling against the EUR, GBP, CHF, and AUD while gaining versus the CAD, JPY, and NZD. Improving sentiment in the Middle East weighed on the greenback as investors embraced a more risk-friendly tone following reports that the United States and Iran have reached a framework peace agreement that is expected to be formally signed on Friday. According to President Trump, the agreement includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade of Iran, a commitment that Iran will not possess a nuclear weapon, and strict enforcement mechanisms. Additional details are expected later this week. With geopolitical tensions easing, market attention is shifting toward a busy week for central banks. The Federal Reserve is expected to leave rates unchanged when it announces its decision on Wednesday, but investors will be focused on Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting at the helm and any clues regarding the future policy bias. Before the Fed decision, the Bank of Japan is widely expected to raise its policy rate by 25 basis points to 1.00% from 0.75%. Market pricing and economist surveys strongly favor a hike as inflation pressures continue to prove more persistent. The BoJ's April meeting featured a hawkish 6-3 vote split, with three members already favoring an immediate rate increase. Since then, inflation forecasts have moved higher and policymakers have expressed growing concern about upside inflation risks. Markets will also be watching for guidance on bond purchases. The expectation is that the BoJ may signal a slower pace of bond-buying tapering beginning in fiscal 2027 while leaving the door open for additional rate hikes later this year. Current market pricing points to rates reaching approximately 1.25% by the fourth quarter. With Governor Kazuo Ueda sidelined due to hospitalization, Deputy Governor Shinichi Uchida is expected to lead the post-meeting press conference. Elsewhere, the Reserve Bank of Australia is expected to leave rates unchanged when it announces its decision Tuesday evening U.S. time. On Thursday, both the Swiss National Bank and the Bank of England are also expected to maintain current policy settings. Economic data released today was mixed. The June Empire State Manufacturing Index fell to 5.7 from 19.6 in May, well below the 14.0 estimate. While the reading remains in expansion territory, it signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing activity in the New York region. New orders and employment remained positive, but growth momentum weakened and price pressures persisted. Industrial production rose 0.1% in May, below expectations for a 0.2% increase. Manufacturing output was unchanged following a strong 0.7% gain in April, suggesting industrial activity continues to expand but at a more modest pace. Strength in mining and AI-related technology production helped offset softer areas of manufacturing, while capacity utilization edged higher to 76.2%. Housing data was also softer than expected. The NAHB Housing Market Index fell to 35 in June from 37 in May, missing expectations. Elevated mortgage rates, affordability concerns, and higher construction costs continue to weigh on builder confidence. Sentiment has now remained below the key 50 level for more than two years, with many builders increasingly relying on incentives and price reductions to attract buyers. U.S. equities rallied sharply as investors welcomed the easing of Middle East tensions. The Nasdaq led the advance, climbing more than 3%. • Dow Jones Industrial Average: 51,676.18, +469.07 points (+0.92%) — a new record close • S&P 500: 7,554.28, +122.83 points (+1.65%) — within reach of the record close of 7,609.77 set on June 2 • Nasdaq Composite: 26,683.94, +795.10 points (+3.07%) — still below its record close of 27,093.90 U.S. Treasury yields were mixed, with shorter maturities modestly lower and longer-term yields moving higher: • 2-year: 4.070%, -1.4 basis points • 5-year: 4.193%, -1.7 basis points • 10-year: 4.475%, -1.0 basis points • 30-year: 4.976%, +3.5 basis points In commodities, crude oil fell sharply as geopolitical risk premiums were reduced. WTI crude declined $3.54, or 4.17%, to $81.34 after trading as low as $79.70 before rebounding. Precious metals moved higher, benefiting from lower short-term yields and ongoing demand for hard assets: • Gold: +$91 (+2.15%) to $4,309 • Silver: +$1.90 (+2.82%) to $69.93 This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 央行政策预期变化可能提升主要货币对波动，杠杆仓位的保证金与回撤压力上升。
items.impactEn: Shifts in central-bank expectations may increase major-pair volatility and raise margin/drawdown pressure on leveraged positions.

items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the Italian final CPI and the German ZEW index. The data won't change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. Moreover, we can expect sentiment indices like the ZEW or PMIs to improve in the next months on easing rate hike expectations and lower energy prices. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the only highlight is the US housing starts and building permits report. This is not market-moving data and won't change anything for the Fed. The markets are now focused on the positive effects stemming from the US-Iran deal and the official end of the war. Expectations of lower oil prices are easing inflation concerns and leading to a dovish repricing in interest rates. The short-term effect is certainly positive for growth and risk assets like stocks are the main beneficiaries. Looking ahead, the question will be whether this negative supply shock turns into a positive demand one where economic activity strengthens significantly and keeps inflationary pressure high eventually forcing the Fed to intervene with rate hikes. This is likely going to be the next tail risk for markets. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 10:55 GMT/06:55 ET - ECB's Escriva (neutral - voter) 13:10 GMT/09:10 ET - ECB's Lane (neutral - voter) 19:05 GMT/15:05 ET - ECB's Sleijpen (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，除了意大利最终CPI和德国ZEW指数等几项低关注度数据外，日程上没有太多值得关注的内容。这些数据不会对欧洲央行的决策产生影响，因此市场反应预计将较为温和。此外，随着加息预期的缓解和能源价格的走低，预计未来几个月ZEW或PMI等市场信心指数将有所改善。美股时段 在美股时段，唯一值得关注的亮点是美国新屋开工和建筑许可报告。这些数据不会引发市场波动，也不会改变美联储的政策走向。目前市场正聚焦于美伊协议带来的积极影响以及战争的正式结束。油价走低的预期正在缓解通胀担忧，并促使市场对利率进行鸽派重估。短期来看，这对经济增长无疑是利好，而股票等风险资产将成为主要受益者。展望未来，关键问题在于：这场负面供给冲击能否转化为积极的需求冲击——即经济活动显著增强，从而维持高通胀压力，最终迫使美联储通过加息进行干预。 这很可能将成为市场面临的下一个尾部风险。央行官员讲话 格林尼治时间10:55/美国东部时间06:55 - 欧洲央行埃斯克里瓦（中性立场 - 投票委员） 格林尼治时间13:10/美国东部时间09:10 - 欧洲央行莱恩（中性立场 - 投票委员） 格林尼治时间19:05/美国东部时间15:05 - 欧洲央行斯莱彭（中性立场 - 投票委员） 本文由investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔撰写。

Moreover, we can expect sentiment indices like the ZEW or PMIs to improve in the next months on easing rate hike expectations and lower energy prices. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the only highlight is the US housing starts and building permits report. This is not market-moving data and won't change anything for the Fed. Looking ahead, the question will be whether this negative supply shock turns into a positive demand one where economic activity strengthens significantly and keeps inflationary pressure high eventually forcing the Fed to intervene with rate hikes. This is likely going to be the next tail risk for markets.
